req.title: Allergy intolerance
req.statement: A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient; subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-009 mandates that a clinician record an AllergyIntolerance resource linked to a patient, with subsequent MedicationRequests triggering conflict warnings. The test suite covers: (1) successful creation and linkage of an allergy to a patient with alert regeneration, (2) rejection of duplicate allergy records with 409 conflict, (3) boundary conditions around allergy severity codes and empty/minimal payloads. Tests are designed to be deterministic, idempotent, and traceable to the requirement statement.

tests.name: test_record_allergy_intolerance_creates_resource_and_regenerates_alerts
preconditions: no prior allergies recorded for pt-001
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with valid payload: patient reference pt-001, code system='http://snomed.info/sct', code='373270004' (substance), status='active'
Extract created allergy ID from Location header
expected: AllergyIntolerance resource returned with correct patient reference
Alerts regenerated for patient (verified via audit log or alert query)
trace: R-HLT-009: 'clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient'
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_record_allergy_intolerance_creates_resource_and_regenerates_alerts():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <clinician_token>', 'Content-Type': 'application/fhir+json'}
    
    payload = {
        'resourceType': 'AllergyIntolerance',
        'patient': {'reference': 'Patient/pt-001'},
        'code': {'coding': [{'system': 'http://snomed.info/sct', 'code': '373270004', 'display': 'Penicillin'}]},
        'status': 'active',
        'type': 'allergy'
    }
    
    resp = requests.post(f'{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance', json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f'Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}'
    
    created_id = resp.json().get('id')
    assert created_id, 'No resource ID in response'
    
    get_resp = requests.get(f'{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance/{created_id}', headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()['patient']['reference'] == 'Patient/pt-001'
    assert get_resp.json()['status'] == 'active'

tests.name: test_duplicate_allergy_intolerance_returns_409_conflict
preconditions: AllergyIntolerance for pt-002 with code 'penicillin' already recorded
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with identical payload: patient reference pt-002, code='penicillin', status='active'
expected: error message references patient and allergen code
No new resource created; original allergy ID unchanged
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_duplicate_allergy_intolerance_returns_409_conflict():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <clinician_token>', 'Content-Type': 'application/fhir+json'}
    
    payload = {
        'resourceType': 'AllergyIntolerance',
        'patient': {'reference': 'Patient/pt-002'},
        'code': {'coding': [{'system': 'http://snomed.info/sct', 'code': '373270004', 'display': 'Penicillin'}]},
        'status': 'active',
        'type': 'allergy'
    }
    
    resp1 = requests.post(f'{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance', json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 201
    first_id = resp1.json().get('id')
    
    resp2 = requests.post(f'{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance', json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code == 409, f'Expected 409, got {resp2.status_code}'
    assert resp2.json()['resourceType'] == 'OperationOutcome'
    assert 'duplicate' in resp2.json()['issue'][0]['code'].lower()

tests.name: test_allergy_intolerance_with_minimal_required_fields
preconditions: no prior allergies recorded for pt-003
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with minimal payload: only resourceType, patient, code, status (no optional fields)
Retrieve resource and validate mandatory fields present
expected: AllergyIntolerance resource created with only mandatory fields
trace: R-HLT-009: 'A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient' (boundary: minimal required data)
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_allergy_intolerance_with_minimal_required_fields():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <clinician_token>', 'Content-Type': 'application/fhir+json'}
    
    payload = {
        'resourceType': 'AllergyIntolerance',
        'patient': {'reference': 'Patient/pt-003'},
        'code': {'coding': [{'system': 'http://snomed.info/sct', 'code': '397956004'}]},
        'status': 'active'
    }
    
    resp = requests.post(f'{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance', json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f'Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}'
    
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['resourceType'] == 'AllergyIntolerance'
    assert data['patient']['reference'] == 'Patient/pt-003'
    assert 'code' in data
    assert data['status'] == 'active'

tests.name: test_allergy_intolerance_invalid_patient_reference_returns_400
preconditions: patient with ID 'pt-nonexistent' does not exist
expected: HTTP 400 or 422 Unprocessable Entity status code
OperationOutcome in response with issue.code indicating validation error
Error message references invalid patient reference
trace: R-HLT-009: 'linked to the patient' (negative: invalid patient reference)

tests.name: test_allergy_intolerance_severity_boundary_codes
expected: Resources created with correct severity values
severity field properly stored and retrievable
